Last year, I was suffering from a severe intestinal infection caused by contaminated food (later correctly diagnosed by a senior doctor). Instead of a systematic approach, I was put on a random diagnostic path.
First, I was told I might have TB and was asked to undergo blood tests. When those came back negative, it was then speculated to be typhoid, despite no exposure history or clinical correlation. I was prescribed strong antibiotics, but my symptoms did not resolve at all. After nearly 20 days of unnecessary suffering, I consulted another senior doctor who correctly diagnosed and treated the condition.

This year, we visited her again for my wife, who had classic viral infection symptoms—cold, cough, and mild fever. I was already hesitant based on my past experience, but there was no other doctor available at Spectra that day and we had an urgent obligation.
The consultation was extremely robotic and superficial. No basic examination was done—no chest, breathing, abdomen, or clinical assessment, which is the bare minimum expected from a general physician. Despite clear viral symptoms, my wife was again prescribed antibiotics for 5 days.
When I questioned whether this could simply be a viral infection, the response was dismissive: “Aap yeh medicines lo, theek ho jayega.” This felt like an assumption that the illness would resolve on its own regardless of what was prescribed.

Overall, the approach felt careless, dismissive, and lacking clinical judgment. Based on repeated personal experience, I do not feel confident recommending this doctor. The quality of medical care and the dedication of most of the hospital staff were truly outstanding, making the overall patient experience highly positive, save for one significant administrative issue during discharge.
​

​Dr. Sachdeva is an exemplary professional. He is highly skilled in his job and stands out for his patient-centric approach. He possesses a remarkably cool and collected demeanor, which is profoundly calming for the patient. Unlike many specialists, he operates without any perceived "attitude," making communication effortless and reassuring. Dr. Sachdeva went above and beyond, assisting us with every query, both medical and non-medical, making us feel completely supported throughout the entire treatment journey.
​

​The hospital facilities and services are good and well-maintained. The Help Desk representatives were helpful and efficient, providing good service.
​The Ground Staff were also largely commendable and performed their duties professionally.

​Major Drawback: The TPA and Discharge Process
​The only substantial issue we encountered was during the discharge process, which was severely hampered by the slow and unsupportive Third Party Administrator (TPA) coordination.

​The TPA department representative, specifically Mr. Murty, was extremely slow in processing the paperwork and lacked coordination in obtaining the necessary approvals from the TPA department. We were scheduled for discharge by noon, but due to continuous coordination and direct intervention with the TPA department, the final approvals were not received until a frustrating 8:00 PM. This eight-hour delay caused significant unnecessary stress and prolonged our hospital stay unnecessarily.
​

​Apollo Spectra Hospital provides high-quality medical services, and we highly recommend Dr. Neetan Sachdeva for his surgical expertise and compassionate care. The majority of the staff, from the help desk to the ground staff, were excellent. Dr. Sabyasachi Bal Sir — words genuinely fall short when it comes to describing him.

He is not just a surgeon…
He is a blessing in human form.

So humble.
So genuine.
So respectful.
So deeply compassionate.

The way he speaks to patients and their families makes you feel like you’re talking to your own family member — not a doctor.

His confidence heals half the pain.
His skills heal the rest.

As a surgeon, he stands at a level very few can ever reach.
His expertise in Rib and Lung Surgery is unmatched — not just in the city, not just in the state, but truly one of the finest in India.

In the toughest moments, when fear takes over and uncertainty surrounds you, Dr. Bal Sir becomes that ray of hope — calm, strong, reassuring.

For us, he isn’t just a doctor.
He is the reason faith feels real.
He is the reason healing feels possible.

A true gem of a person —
A legend in his field —
And a gift to every patient who gets treated by him.

🌿 Respect. Gratitude. Blessings. S. Bhatyal, urologist and surgeon Dr. Ankur Arya, urologist Dr. Siddhant Kumar, and resident doctor Dr. Madhur Shroff — for their high professionalism, attentive care, and for successfully performing two complex surgeries.
Before the surgeries, very thorough examinations were carried out, and the doctors explained everything in detail and always answered all of our questions. The urology team is truly one of the best.
We would especially like to thank Dr. Madhur Shroff, who carefully monitored the patient’s condition in the ICU after surgery, always checked on the patient’s well-being, and performed wound dressings very carefully and with excellent sterility.
Special thanks also to the translators Karuna and Piyush. They met us at the airport, always accompanied us from the hotel to the hospital and back, helped us with everything, and carefully translated all information into Russian.
We are grateful to the nurses, ward assistants, housekeeping staff, and the entire hospital team for their care, help, and cleanliness in the patient rooms. The hospital food was also tasty, but for international patients it would be better to prepare meals with minimal spices or without spices at all.
We would also like to mention that the restrooms in the international lounge were not always clean enough, and in our opinion, more attention should be given to this.
The only thing we did not like was that men, women, and children could sometimes stay together in the same room. In a urology department, it is very important for patients to stay in gender-separated rooms. We appreciate that the staff helped resolve this issue when we requested it.
Once again, we sincerely thank the entire hospital staff for their help, care, and professionalism!

Feedback like yours helps us stay focused on what matters most to our patients, their comfort and well-being.","reply_language":"en","reply_posted_at_unix_micros":1778391534000000,"reply_updated_at_unix_micros":1778391534000000,"published_at":"2026-05-10T05:32:15.210499Z"},{"Name":"Explorer","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV2VaUvDP761_Kv3dy-zz51I640ELaVJqVIzxBonGwVx-rO2sQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"After extensive research, we chose BLK Max Hospital for gynaecology and obstetrics care, and our overall experience was truly commendable.

A heartfelt thanks to Dr. Lakshmi Mantri and Dr. Uma Rani Swain, who are exceptional in their field. Their expertise, calm approach, and transparency in communication immediately instill confidence. The processes they follow reflect deep experience and commitment to patient well-being.

Their supporting team deserves equal appreciation — Dr. Riya, Dr. Monica, Dr. Priyanshi, Dr. Nidhi, and Dr. Manisha — all of whom showed immense understanding, care, and professionalism throughout. The team tried every possible method to ensure a normal delivery, and only after exhausting all safe options did they proceed with a C-section. Their patience and compassion made a big difference.

The supporting staff was equally efficient and kind. The nurses, security staff, feeding assistants, and porter services played a crucial role in making the stay comfortable. Special thanks to:
Ms. Jobina, Ms. Laxmi, Ms. Roseline, Ms. Lidia, Mrs. Sonam, Ms. Khushi, Ms. Sanjana, Mrs. Durgesh, Mrs. Poonam, Mrs. Yashodha, and Mr. Talim. Each of them contributed with genuine care and attention. The billing was professionally and smoothly taken care by Mr. Jhanvi, Mr. Amandeep and Ms. Deepanshi.

The food service was excellent as well — meals were nutritious, timely, and tailored to the patient’s needs. Special mention to Ms. Priyanka and Mr. Kaushal (Supervisor) for their dedicated service to Room No. 401B.

On the technical and medical side, everything was smooth and well-managed. However, the administrative process for bed allocation could be more transparent. It was noticeable that patients under CGHS/DGHS or other panels were not given the same priority as private ones. For example, OPD billing for panel patients starts late, and initially, only one counter is opened until complaints arise. Similarly, for in-patient admissions, private wards are often shown as “booked,” even when available, which can be disappointing for those eligible.

Please note: For CGHS/DGHS/Panel  regular consultations where the doctors are only team doctors/ junior doctors and not Dr. Umarani or Dr. Laxmi madam.

Their roaster changes everytime and hardly you meet the same doctor every time you visit. However, for the final procedure the Dr. Umarani or  Dr. Laxmi Madam will be heading.

While this seems to be a management-level issue, the hospital’s cleanliness, maintenance, and security remain top-notch — the premises are spotless, and staff are courteous and efficient.

Overall, despite minor administrative shortcomings, our medical experience at BLK Max Hospital was outstanding, thanks to the dedication of the doctors, nurses, and support staff. In the face of a challenging and incurable condition, he has worked tirelessly to keep my father stable and comfortable. His unwavering compassion, boundless empathy, and profound medical expertise have been a source of strength not only for my father but for our entire family.

His calm and positive demeanor reassures every patient who walks through his door. He demystifies complex medical information with patience and clarity, putting us at ease and helping us feel healthier simply by being present. His willingness to pick up my countless calls at odd hours and guide me through moments of crisis exceeds what any family could ask from a physician.

While liver cirrhosis offers no cure outside of transplantation, maintaining stability in such severe circumstances is itself a remarkable achievement. Under your care, this feat has seemed effortless. Doctor, your dedication and extraordinary skill have given my father—and by extension, our family—hope, comfort, and relief beyond measure.

On 10th October 2025, I met with a road accident in which my right foot was severely injured under my two-wheeler. I was unable to stand properly or put any pressure on my foot.

On a friend’s recommendation, I visited AO Specialty Clinic, where I consulted Dr. Kamal Bachani. He patiently listened to my concerns and conducted a thorough examination. He accurately diagnosed chip fragmentation in my right foot along with compressed and swollen tendons. His explanation of the condition and treatment plan was very clear and reassuring.

Dr. Bachani recommended a 21-day Belloni plaster and provided supportive accessories that allowed me to walk with some difficulty, but importantly, I was able to continue attending my office. He monitored my recovery closely throughout the treatment. Once the fragmented area began healing and tissue development was observed, he removed the plaster and referred me to Dr. Nancy Sharma (PT) for physiotherapy.

From my very first physiotherapy session, Dr. Nancy Sharma was extremely professional, attentive, and thorough. She carefully assessed my condition, identified the exact pain points, and designed a well-structured treatment plan with targeted exercises. What truly stood out was her patience and constant encouragement—she ensured I understood every exercise and regularly checked on my comfort during each session.

Thanks to her expertise and dedication, the swelling and pain in my right foot have significantly reduced, and I have regained both mobility and confidence in my daily activities. I am deeply grateful for her compassionate approach and commitment to patient recovery.

I highly recommend Dr. Kamal Bachani and Dr. Nancy Sharma (PT) to anyone seeking effective orthopedic care and physiotherapy for long-term recovery. My experience at Max Hospital Saket was extremely disappointing. The staff behaved in a very irresponsible manner, and there seemed to be no value for patients’ time at all. Despite having an appointment, I was made to wait unnecessarily, and no one bothered to give clear updates.

What made it worse was the attitude of the staff. Instead of helping or guiding patients politely, many of them acted as if they were doing a favour. The lack of basic courtesy and professionalism was shocking for a hospital of this level.

Overall, the management seriously needs to improve their staff behavior, accountability, and respect for patients’ time. I visited the Gate 5 Emergency Ward due to a leg injury. Since it was an emergency case and the hospital charges premium fees for almost everything, I expected a prompt and organized process. Unfortunately, my experience was completely opposite.

Upon arrival, I informed the staff that I was in severe pain and needed immediate attention. I was simply asked to sit and wait. After about 30 minutes, a staff member came to take my BP and ask a few basic questions. That was all.

After nearly 1.5 hours, another person came, asked a routine question about the injury without even examining it, and left. After waiting for more than 2.5 hours, I approached the staff again to ask about the next steps. I was told to keep waiting. Only after repeatedly emphasizing my pain did someone come and ask me to complete emergency registration at the counter.

Following registration, I waited another 30–40 minutes before being taken for an X-ray. After the X-ray, I was brought back to the same waiting area and left there for another 40–50 minutes. Once again, I had to approach the staff myself to ask what was happening. I was told they were waiting for the X-ray report.

About 30 minutes later, a staff member suddenly asked me to go to reception for discharge. I paid approximately ₹2,800 and returned, expecting a doctor to explain the diagnosis and treatment plan. Surprisingly, no doctor spoke to me. A staff member simply told me to take the medicines mentioned in the discharge summary. I wasn't even given the X-ray report.

I later noticed that a doctor consultation fee of ₹700 had been charged. When I asked where the doctor was, I was informed that the previous doctor's shift had ended and that I should consult another doctor myself. While still in significant pain, I approached the doctor, who finally explained the diagnosis after reading the report. I also had to ask separately about collecting the X-ray, only to learn that it was available at the cardiology reception.

Overall, there was almost no communication throughout the process. Patients are expected to chase staff for every update and every step of the treatment. For an emergency visit, spending 5–6 hours with such poor coordination and communication is simply unacceptable.

Extremely disappointing experience.","Images":null,"When":"a month ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xKemMwVTNaRU52WnpBM2JrdE9MVXB6YUVSbmNHYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/114073547036942960343/reviews?hl=en-GB","posted_at_unix_micros":1780387520242982,"updated_at_unix_micros":1780387520242982,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"Very poor management and service.

At that time I didn’t go for the surgery bcz my daughter was just 6 months old. I waited patiently and didn’t pursue for the surgery.
But on 26 jan 2026 a severe pain took place in my upper abdomen. I was able to realise that it’s the pain caused by stones. Surprisingly, the pain subsidised on its own and I joined my work next day. But, something serious was about to happen,  I started with itching badly and it was becoming worse day by day. Then I booked my blood tests on 30 jan 2026. The results were shocking. The LFT profile was grossly increased which left me and my family in shock. Next morning I visited Dr. Vivek Raj and he explained everything very calmly and advised me to go for MIR. I did that test and we found that three stones were stuck in my bile duct. I got admitted next day and Dr. Vivek Raj and his team took so good care of me. Dr. Vivek Raj and his team performed ERCP. The process was so smooth. I would like to extent my gratitude to every one in his team who made the entire process very very comfortable to me.
